figures.caption: FIG. 4. Reconstruction of the transverse velocity component v for the Kovasznay flow. In each row, from left to right: reference solution, prediction, and absolute error. The four rows correspond to (a) Nu_learn, (b) Nu_off, (c) ResAttn, and (d) GRU, respectively.

FIG. 2. Pressure-field reconstruction for the Kovasznay flow. In each row, from left to right: reference solution, prediction, and absolute error. The four rows correspond to (a) Nu_learn, (b) Nu_off, (c) ResAttn, and (d) GRU, respectively.

FIG. 3. Reconstruction of the streamwise velocity component u for the Kovasznay flow. In each row, from left to right: reference solution, prediction, and absolute error. The four rows correspond to (a) Nu_learn, (b) Nu_off, (c) ResAttn, and (d) GRU, respectively.

FIG. 8. Reconstruction of the transverse velocity component v for manufactured forcing flow I. In each row, from left to right: reference solution, prediction, and absolute error. The four rows correspond to (a) Nu_learn, (b) Nu_off, (c) ResAttn, and (d) GRU, respectively.

FIG. 6. Pressure-field reconstruction for manufactured forcing flow I. In each row, from left to right: reference solution, prediction, and absolute error. The four rows correspond to (a) Nu_learn, (b) Nu_off, (c) ResAttn, and (d) GRU, respectively.

FIG. 7. Reconstruction of the streamwise velocity component u for manufactured forcing flow I. In each row, from left to right: reference solution, prediction, and absolute error. The four rows correspond to (a) Nu_learn, (b) Nu_off, (c) ResAttn, and (d) GRU, respectively.
